title: What Is Tenant Representation — And Why Every Newcastle Business Needs It
excerpt: Most commercial leases are negotiated entirely in the landlord's favour. Here's what tenant representation is, what it costs, and why it changes the outcome.
author: Joe Kelley
metaTitle: Tenant Representation Newcastle | Your Office Space
metaDescription: What is commercial tenant representation and why does it matter in Newcastle? Joe Kelley explains how tenant rep works, what it costs, and what you get.
body:
## The problem with signing a commercial lease without help

When a Newcastle business signs a commercial lease, there are typically two parties at the table: the landlord, and the landlord's agent. The landlord's agent is paid by the landlord, owes their duty to the landlord, and has done this hundreds of times before.

The business owner, signing a lease for the first time in five years, has usually done almost no preparation.

This is not a fair fight. And the outcome of that negotiation will affect the business's occupancy cost, flexibility, and exposure for the next three to seven years.

## What tenant representation actually is

A tenant representative — or tenant rep — works exclusively for the tenant. Not the landlord. Not the building manager. The tenant.

That means:

- **We find the right space** — not just the one that's available, but the one that fits your brief, your budget, and your team
- **We negotiate the lease** — rent, incentives, outgoings, rent review mechanisms, options, make-good, assignment rights
- **We protect your interests at every clause** — leases are long documents full of risk. We read them properly so you don't have to
- **We ensure incentives are maximised** — landlords routinely offer fitout contributions, rent-free periods, and other incentives. If you don't ask, they don't offer

## How tenant rep is typically charged

At Your Office Space, our fee for tenant representation is $12,000 ex GST or the equivalent of three months rent — whichever is higher.

This is paid by the tenant, which is exactly the point. When your advisor is paid by the landlord, there is an inherent conflict of interest. When they're paid by you, their only job is to get you the best possible outcome.

In practice, the incentives we negotiate — fitout contributions, rent reductions, rent-free periods — regularly exceed our fee by a significant margin. Most clients recover our cost within the first year of the lease.

## The Newcastle and Hunter Valley commercial market in 2026

The current Newcastle CBD and suburban commercial market has vacancy at around 8–12% depending on the precinct and asset class. That gives tenants genuine leverage — if they know how to use it.

Landlords in the current market are generally willing to offer:

- **Fitout contributions** of $50–$200/m² for quality tenants on reasonable terms
- **Rent-free periods** of 1–4 months on a standard 3–5 year lease
- **Cap rate escalations** in lieu of uncapped market reviews

If you're not negotiating these, you're leaving real money on the table.

## What happens if you sign without a tenant rep

We see the same pattern regularly. A business owner finds a space they like, meets the landlord's agent, gets a lease, runs it past their solicitor for a legal check, and signs.

The solicitor confirms the lease is legally valid. But no one has negotiated the commercial terms. The rent is face rent. There's no incentive. The rent reviews are uncapped market reviews. The make-good clause requires full reinstatement. The personal guarantee is unlimited.

All of that was negotiable. None of it was negotiated.

## Who should use a tenant rep

Any business signing or renewing a commercial lease in Newcastle or the Hunter Valley. The higher the rent or the longer the term, the more important it becomes. But even smaller deals benefit — a 200m² office at $350/m² is a $70,000 per year commitment. That deserves proper representation.

## Ready to talk?

If you're about to sign a lease, renew a lease, or start looking for commercial space in Newcastle or the Hunter Valley, talk to us first. The first conversation is always free — and it costs nothing to know what your options are before you commit.
